title: Agent monitoring: watch-loop support + passive hook + consumption instructions
description:
FIRST STEP: 'git merge main --no-edit' in the worktree; verify /api/comment + kb.py comments exist.

Make the channel agent-consumable:
1. Ensure 'kb.py comments list --json --since <cursor>' returns open comments after a cursor (for an active watch-loop) — if not already from T1, add it.
2. Add a UserPromptSubmit hook script/snippet (assets/templates) that prints pending open comments as a context block (PASSIVE monitor). Provide it as a template; actual install is T4/init.
3. Document the AGENT CONSUMPTION PROTOCOL in references/web.md (or a new references/comments.md): when a comment arrives, treat it as actionable user feedback anchored to {page, section, selected_text} — the agent decides: propose a page edit (propose-only), answer the user, or file to inbox/; then 'kb.py comments resolve <id>'. Also document ACTIVE WATCH MODE: on 'watch the wiki for comments', the agent enters a poll loop (ScheduleWakeup/Monitor) calling 'kb.py comments list --json --since <cursor>' on a short interval and acting on new ones until told to stop. Add a one-line pointer in the CLAUDE.md shim + repo-wiki/INDEX.md.

This touches kb.py (maybe), a hook template, and docs — disjoint from T2's client files.

Test: kb.py comments list --json --since <id> returns only newer comments; the hook template prints pending comments given a sample channel file; docs describe both modes.
notes: 2026-06-14 15:32 - [human] AGENT UPDATES THE WIKI: a comment may ask for a change. The agent acts by EDITING the .md file directly (no server write endpoint) — the comment is the human-in-the-loop approval. Protocol: open <page> at <line>, apply the change, append a Timeline entry + bump verified_against if appropriate, then 'kb.py comments resolve <id> --note'. The viewer auto-refreshes (see the new live-refresh tick) so the user sees the edit. Document this in the consumption protocol.
acceptance_criteria: comments list --json --since works; a UserPromptSubmit hook template injects pending comments; consumption + watch-mode protocol documented; shim/INDEX pointer added
closed_reason: Completed: comments-hook.sh (UserPromptSubmit passive monitor), references/comments.md (watch-loop + act-then-resolve protocol), shim+INDEX pointers, SKILL.md section. Hook prints pending comments; silent when none.
